sn35.242:0.3: The Simile of the Tree Trunk (2nd)
sn35.242:1.1:
On one occasion the Blessed One was dwelling at Kimbila on the bank of the river Ganges. The Blessed One saw a great log being carried along by the current of the river Ganges, and he addressed the bhikkhus thus: "Do you see, bhikkhus,
that great log being carried along by the current of the river Ganges?"
"Yes, venerable sir." …
…
When this was said, the Venerable Kimbila asked the Blessed One: "What, venerable sir, is the near shore … what is inward rottenness?"
"And what, Kimbila, is inward rottenness? Here, Kimbila, a bhikkhu commits a certain defiled offence, an offence of a kind that does not allow for rehabilitation.
This is called inward rottenness."
